title: Meet the Advisors | NYU Shanghai
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU New York City.
text:
Meet the Advisors
Chengcheng FengEmail:chengcheng.f@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Computer Science; Data Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems Engineering Chengcheng Feng obtained her master’s degree in Higher Education at the University of Pennsylvania, and her bachelor’s degree in Early Childhood Education from the University of Minnesota-Twin Cities. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Chengcheng worked in the Office of University Registrar at the University of Pennsylvania. Chengcheng also had experience working in the International Student and Scholar Services at the University of Minnesota-Twin Cities, providing transition support to international students from diverse countries.
Email:chengcheng.f@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Computer Science; Data Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems Engineering
Chengcheng Feng obtained her master’s degree in Higher Education at the University of Pennsylvania, and her bachelor’s degree in Early Childhood Education from the University of Minnesota-Twin Cities. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Chengcheng worked in the Office of University Registrar at the University of Pennsylvania. Chengcheng also had experience working in the International Student and Scholar Services at the University of Minnesota-Twin Cities, providing transition support to international students from diverse countries.
Huimei (Jenny) WangEmail: jennyw@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentSpecialization: Data Science; Computer Science; Mathematics; Honors Mathematics; Inbound Study Away Students Jenny Wang completed her master’s degree in Social Service Administration from the University of Chicago, and her bachelor’s degree in Math and Economics from Emory and Henry College. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Jenny worked at the United Nations and the University of Chicago.
Email: jennyw@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Specialization: Data Science; Computer Science; Mathematics; Honors Mathematics; Inbound Study Away Students
Jenny Wang completed her master’s degree in Social Service Administration from the University of Chicago, and her bachelor’s degree in Math and Economics from Emory and Henry College. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Jenny worked at the United Nations and the University of Chicago.
Jing ZhouEmail: jing.zhou@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Business & Finance; Business & Marketing Jing Zhou completed her master’s degree in Developmental Psychology and Education from University of Toronto, and her bachelor’s degree in Psychology from Vancouver Island University. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Jing worked as an education consultant at a top British education consulting company in Shanghai. She was also involved in working with various school districts in Shanghai to promote students’ social and emotional wellbeing.
Email: jing.zhou@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Jing Zhou completed her master’s degree in Developmental Psychology and Education from University of Toronto, and her bachelor’s degree in Psychology from Vancouver Island University. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Jing worked as an education consultant at a top British education consulting company in Shanghai. She was also involved in working with various school districts in Shanghai to promote students’ social and emotional wellbeing.
Jinglin GuoEmail: jinglin.guo@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Interactive Media Arts; Interactive Media and Business Jinglin Guo received her master’s degree in Management and Information Systems from Western Oregon University in the US, and her bachelor’s degree in Accounting (CPA) from Jiangxi University of Finance and Economics in China. Before joining NYU Shanghai, Jinglin worked for six years in the Academic Advising and Learning Center at Western Oregon University as an international learning specialist and also taught an International First Year Experience class.
Email: jinglin.guo@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Interactive Media Arts; Interactive Media and Business
Jinglin Guo received her master’s degree in Management and Information Systems from Western Oregon University in the US, and her bachelor’s degree in Accounting (CPA) from Jiangxi University of Finance and Economics in China. Before joining NYU Shanghai, Jinglin worked for six years in the Academic Advising and Learning Center at Western Oregon University as an international learning specialist and also taught an International First Year Experience class.
Minqing WangEmail:minqing.wang@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Economics; Mathematics; Honors Mathematics Minqing Wang earned her master’s degree in Education from Harvard University, focusing on linguistics and developmental psychology. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she served as a counselor at Harvard University’s Summer School, providing academic and personal development counseling for students. Minqing was also an instructor at Hult International Business School in Boston.
Email:minqing.wang@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Economics; Mathematics; Honors Mathematics
Minqing Wang earned her master’s degree in Education from Harvard University, focusing on linguistics and developmental psychology. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she served as a counselor at Harvard University’s Summer School, providing academic and personal development counseling for students. Minqing was also an instructor at Hult International Business School in Boston.
Molly (Jiaqi) ZhaoEmail: jiaqi.zhao@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Biology; Chemistry; Neural Science; Physics; Computer Science; Data ScienceMolly Zhao obtained her master’s degree in Educational Counseling at University of Southern California, and her bachelor’s degree in Education Science at University of California, Irvine. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Molly worked at Viterbi School of Engineering of USC as an assistant director of student services for graduate programs.
Email: jiaqi.zhao@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Biology; Chemistry; Neural Science; Physics; Computer Science; Data Science
Molly Zhao obtained her master’s degree in Educational Counseling at University of Southern California, and her bachelor’s degree in Education Science at University of California, Irvine. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Molly worked at Viterbi School of Engineering of USC as an assistant director of student services for graduate programs.
Qinmi (Sammi) LiEmail: sammi.li@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Business & Finance; Business & Marketing Qinmi (Sammi) Li received her master's degree in Economics from San Francisco State University, and her bachelor's degree in Public Finance from Xiamen University. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she worked as a curriculum coordinator at Wenzhou-Kean University, responsible for curriculum scheduling. She was also involved in various business programs as an assistant to Dean of Business.
Email: sammi.li@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Qinmi (Sammi) Li received her master's degree in Economics from San Francisco State University, and her bachelor's degree in Public Finance from Xiamen University. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, she worked as a curriculum coordinator at Wenzhou-Kean University, responsible for curriculum scheduling. She was also involved in various business programs as an assistant to Dean of Business.
Tong JiangEmail: tong.jiang@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Interactive Media Arts; Interactive Media and Business Tong Jiang received her master's degree in TESOL from the University of Pennsylvania and her bachelor's degree in Educational Studies from Saint Louis University. Before joining the NYU Shanghai Advising team, she worked as a language instructor at Upenn and accumulated her teaching experience targeting students from a range of age levels and cultural backgrounds.
Email: tong.jiang@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Interactive Media Arts; Interactive Media and Business
Tong Jiang received her master's degree in TESOL from the University of Pennsylvania and her bachelor's degree in Educational Studies from Saint Louis University. Before joining the NYU Shanghai Advising team, she worked as a language instructor at Upenn and accumulated her teaching experience targeting students from a range of age levels and cultural backgrounds.
Xiao LiangEmail: xiao.liang@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Global China Studies; Business & Finance; Business & MarketingXiao Liang received his master's degree in TESOL from American University and his bachelor's degree in Broadcasting and Hosting from Communication University of Zhejiang. Before joining the Advising team, he worked at NYU Shanghai as a Go Local Academic Advisor. Xiao also had experience teaching and tutoring international students while completing his master's degree.
Email: xiao.liang@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Global China Studies; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Xiao Liang received his master's degree in TESOL from American University and his bachelor's degree in Broadcasting and Hosting from Communication University of Zhejiang. Before joining the Advising team, he worked at NYU Shanghai as a Go Local Academic Advisor. Xiao also had experience teaching and tutoring international students while completing his master's degree.
Xiaoning (Linda) FanEmail:xf960@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Computer Science; Data Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems EngineeringLinda Fan received her master's degree in Education Policy Analysis from Harvard University, and her bachelor's degree in Psychology and Economics from University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Linda worked at Duke Kunshan University as an Admissions Officer.
Email:xf960@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Computer Science; Data Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems Engineering
Linda Fan received her master's degree in Education Policy Analysis from Harvard University, and her bachelor's degree in Psychology and Economics from University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Linda worked at Duke Kunshan University as an Admissions Officer.
Xin (Gabie) ChenEmailgabie.chen@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentSpecialization: Economics; Business & Finance; Business & MarketingGabie Chen received her master’s degree in Developmental Psychology from Teachers College, Columbia University, and her bachelor’s degree in Education and Family Studies from the University of Washington. Prior to joining NYUSH, Gabie worked as a curriculum developer to promote the psychosocial well-being of the youth population in the Pacific Northwest Area and in rural China.
Emailgabie.chen@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Specialization: Economics; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Gabie Chen received her master’s degree in Developmental Psychology from Teachers College, Columbia University, and her bachelor’s degree in Education and Family Studies from the University of Washington. Prior to joining NYUSH, Gabie worked as a curriculum developer to promote the psychosocial well-being of the youth population in the Pacific Northwest Area and in rural China.
Xinyuan ShenEmail:xinyuan.shen@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Biology; Chemistry; Neural Science; Physics; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing Xinyuan received her master’s degree in TESOL from University of Pennsylvania, and a bachelor’s degree in English Translation with a minor in Germanistics from Shanghai International Studies University. Before joining the Advising team, Xinyuan worked at NYU Shanghai as a Global Academic Writing and Speaking Fellow and Coordinator of Academic Support Program in the Academic Resource Center. Xinyuan was also an English instructor at NYUSPS, teaching listening and speaking skills to EFL learners.
Email:xinyuan.shen@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Biology; Chemistry; Neural Science; Physics; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Xinyuan received her master’s degree in TESOL from University of Pennsylvania, and a bachelor’s degree in English Translation with a minor in Germanistics from Shanghai International Studies University. Before joining the Advising team, Xinyuan worked at NYU Shanghai as a Global Academic Writing and Speaking Fellow and Coordinator of Academic Support Program in the Academic Resource Center. Xinyuan was also an English instructor at NYUSPS, teaching listening and speaking skills to EFL learners.
Xixi NiEmail:xixi.ni@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Computer Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems Engineering Xixi Ni received her master’s degree in International Higher Education from Boston College with a focus on student development and international learning and teaching. She obtained her B.A. in Anthropology and International Economics from the University of Richmond. Before joining NYU Shanghai, Xixi worked as a career assistant at MIT providing one-on-one career counseling and professional development workshops to undergraduate and graduate students.
Email:xixi.ni@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Computer Science; Electrical and Systems Engineering; Computer Systems Engineering
Xixi Ni received her master’s degree in International Higher Education from Boston College with a focus on student development and international learning and teaching. She obtained her B.A. in Anthropology and International Economics from the University of Richmond. Before joining NYU Shanghai, Xixi worked as a career assistant at MIT providing one-on-one career counseling and professional development workshops to undergraduate and graduate students.
Yihan (Elaine) LiuEmail: yl98@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Humanities; Social Science; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing Elaine Liu completed her master’s degree in English Education from the Chinese University of Hong Kong, and her bachelor’s degree in English Education from the Education University of Hong Kong. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Elaine worked as a teacher in Hong Kong. From 2019 to 2021, she also worked at SPS office at NYU Shanghai and Go Local Advising Academic Advisor.
Email: yl98@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Humanities; Social Science; Business & Finance; Business & Marketing
Elaine Liu completed her master’s degree in English Education from the Chinese University of Hong Kong, and her bachelor’s degree in English Education from the Education University of Hong Kong. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Elaine worked as a teacher in Hong Kong. From 2019 to 2021, she also worked at SPS office at NYU Shanghai and Go Local Advising Academic Advisor.
Yiren RuanEmail: yiren.ruan@nyu.edu | Schedule an AppointmentMajor Specialization: Social ScienceYiren Ruan completed her master’s degree in International Education Development from Teachers College, Columbia University, and her bachelor’s degree in NYU Shanghai as the inaugural class. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Yiren worked at the State University of New York’s Global Affairs Program. She also worked as the academic advisor for the NYU Shanghai Go Local Program from Fall 2020 to Fall 2021.
Email: yiren.ruan@nyu.edu | Schedule an Appointment
Major Specialization: Social Science
Yiren Ruan completed her master’s degree in International Education Development from Teachers College, Columbia University, and her bachelor’s degree in NYU Shanghai as the inaugural class. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Yiren worked at the State University of New York’s Global Affairs Program. She also worked as the academic advisor for the NYU Shanghai Go Local Program from Fall 2020 to Fall 2021.
